steps.text: A margin of error defines a symmetric range around the sample statistic. With sample mean 52 and margin of error 4, the confidence interval is $$52 \pm 4$$
Compute the bounds: lower = $52 - 4 = 48$, upper = $52 + 4 = 56$. The interval is $[48, 56]$.
The most appropriate conclusion is that the population mean lies within this interval, not definitively above or below 52.

• A — “Less than 52” ignores values from 52 to 56 that are in the interval. Eliminated.
  • B — “Greater than 52” ignores values from 48 to 52 that are in the interval. Eliminated.
  • C — “Between 48 and 56” matches exactly $52 \pm 4$. That confirms C is correct.
  • D — “Less than 48 or greater than 56” describes values outside the interval. Eliminated.
